summary: Iteration 3 — delete-bug fix and 'Archive' / 'Actives' filter validated. Backend: 6/6 new pytest tests pass on /api/notes?status=active|done|todo and DELETE regression (active+done==all partition verified, done excluded from active list). Frontend (mobile 390x844): filter chips filter-status-active (default 'Actives'), -todo, -in_progress, -done ('Archive'), -all ('Tout') all render and switch the list correctly. Empirically active(25)+done(2)=all(27) — perfect partition on Devis category. Note detail: tapping delete-btn opens ConfirmDialog with confirm-dialog-cancel and confirm-dialog-confirm; cancel closes the modal AND keeps the note (verified with GET 200 after cancel); confirm calls DELETE /api/notes/{id} -> 200 and the note returns 404 afterwards. status-done PATCH succeeds, note becomes done in DB and disappears from /api/notes?status=active. Regression: status-todo, status-in_progress, toggle-urgent all functional via UI.

react_native_issues.issue: Pre-existing cosmetic warnings: 'shadow* style props are deprecated. Use boxShadow' and 'props.pointerEvents is deprecated'. Carried over from earlier iterations, not blocking.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py list_notes: special-cases status='active' as {$ne: 'done'} while keeping exact match for 'todo'/'in_progress'/'done'. Clean and unambiguous.

ConfirmDialog.tsx is a proper cross-platform Modal with backdrop tap-to-cancel (Pressable backdrop + e.stopPropagation on inner box) and disabled cancel while deleting (onCancel guarded by !deleting in caller). Solid replacement for Alert.alert.
note/[id].tsx: performDelete sets deleting=true, calls deleteNote, closes modal, then router.back(). On error it resets deleting and closes modal before showing Alert — good UX.

Auto-navigate-back on status='done' uses setTimeout(router.back, 350) — gives the segmented control a visual frame to confirm the change. Note: when there is no back-stack (e.g. deep-link), router.back() emits 'GO_BACK was not handled' dev warning; in normal in-app navigation (list→detail→done) this path is fine.
notes.tsx default StatusFilter is 'active' so done notes are hidden from main list immediately on app open (matches the bug ask).
All required testIDs present: filter-status-{active|todo|in_progress|done|all}, delete-btn, confirm-dialog-confirm, confirm-dialog-cancel, status-{todo|in_progress|done}, toggle-urgent.
seed_data_creation: Each test creates and deletes its own TEST_-prefixed notes; nothing leaked.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Public URL: https://smart-capture-ai-2.preview.emergentagent.com (frontend/.env EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL). Reuse /app/backend/tests/test_status_active_filter.py for delete + active/done filter regression. Pre-existing /app/backend/tests/test_smart_notes.py CRUD POST tests are stale (POST returns List[Note]) — flagged in iter2, still untouched. Filter chip testIDs: filter-status-{active,todo,in_progress,done,all}. Confirm dialog testIDs: confirm-dialog-confirm, confirm-dialog-cancel. When testing auto-back on status='done', start from /notes screen and tap into the note (NOT direct deep-link) so router has a back-stack, otherwise expo-router emits 'GO_BACK not handled' (cosmetic dev warning).
rca_of_issue: Iteration 3 fixed two regressions: (1) Alert.alert on react-native-web has known broken callbacks; replaced by custom Modal-based ConfirmDialog. Verified via UI: cancel keeps the note (GET 200), confirm performs DELETE→200 and GET→404. (2) Done notes were polluting the main list. Backend now supports status='active' = {$ne:'done'} and frontend defaults to 'Actives'; partition verified active+done==all on a real category.
